_meta.note: Webflow MCP returns a FLAT list of nodes (text + image + component-instance) with class names but does NOT expose the parent-child DOM structure. To rebuild the layout faithfully we need the rendered HTML (publish + curl) or a Webflow Code Export ZIP.

sections: Studio info block (STUDIO label, NEW INTERNATIONAL PICTURE COMPANY, LOCATION NEW YORK NY, SEASON 2026, STATUS OPERATIONAL)
Service tags ([F] FILM, [C] COMMERCIAL, [M] MUSIC VIDEO, [D] DOCUMENTARY)
Value prop paragraph (NIPC IS A POST-PRODUCTION STUDIO SPECIALIZING IN EDITORIAL...)
Editor index header (NO. EDITOR ROLE Client(s) Year)
Editor Profile (uses Editor Detail Roll component aef2a072-8732-9b67-bc8f-9cda28e69f7c)
Drag indicator (◀︎ drag to see more ▶︎)
Footer with contact info and social links
Version footer (VER.VON: 2.0.24, LAST UPDATE: 01.14.2026, STATUS: OPERATIONAL)
placeholders: Many work cards have placeholder text: 'NIKE | Artist name Name', 'Cool Project', 'Carbin', year '63281'
Footer text 'VER.VON: 2.0.24' is fake version string
Editor Index 'TOTAL ENTRIES: 13' matches the actual Editors collection count
openQuestions: The draft Home page in Webflow appears to still be in WIP state with placeholder copy in several places — is the design intent finalized or are you still iterating?
Without a Code Export or staging URL, faithful layout reconstruction requires reading the Webflow CSS class system and inferring structure — this is best-effort, not pixel-perfect.
